heading: Repealed. –(20),Aug. 2, 1956, ch. 887, § 4(a)(16)70 Stat. 947
label: 53 to 57 Repealed. –(20),Aug. 2, 1956, ch. 887, § 4(a)(16)70 Stat. 947
notes: Section 53, R.S. § 2687, provided for apportionment of compensation according to time served. See, generally, sections 5504 and 6101 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
Section 54, R.S. § 2646, related to books to be furnished to collectors and other officers.

Section 55, R.S. § 2647; acts , ; , title IV, § 523, , provided that collectors of customs, and comptrollers and surveyors performing functions of collectors, should render quarterly accounts to Secretary of the Treasury of fines collected, moneys received as rents, etc.Aug. 24, 1912, ch. 35537 Stat. 434June 17, 1930, ch. 49746 Stat. 740
Section 56, R.S. § 2944, related to additional hours of service at public stores in New York.
Section 57, R.S. § 2648; , , related to sale of blanks by collectors and surveyors.act Aug. 24, 1912, ch. 355, § 137 Stat. 434
